title: Smoky Flaky Thai Dried Bean Stir-fry
description: This low-sodium-friendly smoky and flaky Thai dish features tenderized dried white beans that are stir-fried to perfection with a blend of aromatic spices and veggies for a comforting meal.

ingredients.name: 1 cup of drained and rinsed dried white beans
ingredients.notes: Soaked overnight to reduce soaking time

instructions: Place the soaked beans in a pot with water and soak for at least an hour.
Drain and rinse the beans. In a large skillet, sauté garlic and ginger until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add red bell peppers and onion to the skillet and cook for another 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Push vegetables to one side of the pan and add drained beans to the other side. Pour in soy sauce, coconut aminos, lime juice, and smoked paprika over the beans. Stir well.
Fold the sautéed vegetables into the beans, then stir gently until everything is evenly coated with the sauce.
Add salt if desired, adjust seasoning according to taste. Cook for another 5 minutes or until the beans are tender but still slightly firm.
Transfer the dish onto a serving plate and garnish with fresh herbs like cilantro or green onions if available.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
